title: Morocco battle back to claim thumping World Cup win in six-goal thriller with Haiti
original_text:
Morocco confirmed their progress to the World Cup last 32 ‚ÅÝbut twice had to ‚ÅÝcome from behind to ‚Äãget the better of plucky Haiti 4-2 in an action-packed game on Wednesday, and fell short of topping Brazil in the Group C standings .

Morocco finished level on seven points with ‚ÅÝthe five-time world champions, who beat Scotland 3-0, but are runners-up on goal difference and will play the Group F winners ‚Äì either Japan, the Netherlands or Sweden ‚Äì on Monday in the first knockout round.

‚ÄúWe were looking for first spot in the group, but Haiti had ‚Äånothing to lose and played a good transition game, so it was not an easy game for us,‚Äù coach Mohamed Ouahbi said.

‚ÄúMorocco has entered a whole new ‚ÅÝdimension, and we‚Äôre part ‚ÅÝof a new ‚Äãmomentum where we actually need to believe we can take the title.

‚ÄúHow do we reach this objective? Well, again, as I said before, we need to be 100% committed. Today, we have the best ingredients ‚ÅÝthat are needed to become the best nation, and we believe we can do it; the players ‚Äãbelieve in it, the same for the ‚Äåstaff.‚Äù

Haiti, the ‚ÅÝfirst side eliminated from the 48-team tournament after losing their second group game, went ahead early but African footballer of the ‚Äãyear Achraf Hakimi equalised in the 39th minute.

Wilson Isidor‚Äôs thunderbolt ‚Äårestored Haiti‚Äôs lead in the 43rd ‚Äåminute but matters were soon level again as Ismael Saibari scored his third goal in as many World Cup appearances.

It took ‚Äåuntil the 78th minute for Morocco to go ahead when substitute Soufiane Rahimi thrashed home from close range after Haiti failed to clear a corner and 20-year-old Gessime Yassine made it 4-2 in the last minute.

Haiti had only pride to play for but made Morocco work for their victory and despite losing all three games at the tournament ‚Äì their first in 52 years ‚Äì will depart with their reputation enhanced.

‚ÄúWe showed that we didn‚Äôt steal our spot here,‚Äù manager Sebastien Migne said. ‚ÄúWe deserve to be here.

‚ÄúI hope that what ‚Äåwe gave the fans was good enough for them. Unfortunately, we didn‚Äôt get a point, we would have loved to give at least a point to our fans.‚Äù

With vociferous support willing them forward, Haiti opened the scoring after 10 ‚Äåminutes as Josu√© Casimir calmly shielded the ball, waiting for Jean-K√©vin Duverne to support him on the wing and carry it into the box before a square pass was audaciously backheeled goalwards by Lenny ‚ÄãJoseph.

The goal was initially awarded to the Haiti striker even though his effort looked to be going wide, deflecting in off the back of Morocco goalkeeper Yassine Bounou, but it was later changed to become yet another own goal in this tournament.

Haiti‚Äôs 38-year-old goalkeeper Johny Placide, playing his last international after 15 years in the team, produced a series of sharp saves to twice deny Ayoub ‚ÅÝEl Kaabi and Hakimi. But when he parried Bilal El Khannouss‚Äôs cross in the 39th minute, Hakimi ‚Äãwas quickest to bundle it over the ‚Äãline for the equaliser.

Duverne set up Isidor ‚Äãfor his rasping strike from outside the area to put Haiti ahead again.

Morocco‚Äôs response was much quicker ‚Äãthe second time, with Sofyan ‚ÄåAmrabat setting Hakimi off ‚Äãdown the right flank and ‚Äãthe captain dragging the ball back for Saibari to slot home.

Haiti‚Äôs defences were eventually breached from a set-piece although they protested vehemently for a foul after Rahimi had controlled the ball before turning and slamming home. Rahimi set up Yassine for a tap-in after Haiti‚Äôs defenders stopped playing, believing the ball had gone out of play, but VAR confirmed the goal.
